description: Version value object parses YY.M format correctly
then: Returns a Version with year=26, month=3, patch=0

description: Version value object parses YY.M.patch format
then: Returns a Version with year=26, month=3, patch=1

description: Version comparison orders by year then month then patch
hex --version prints the current CLI version
given: hex CLI is installed with package.json version '26.3'
then: Prints 'hex 26.3' to stdout and exits with code 0

description: Version adapter reads hex-hub binary version
then: Returns the version from the binary (via --version flag or Cargo.toml)

description: Version adapter returns null when no hex-hub binary found
given: No hex-hub binary on any search path

description: CLI warns when hex-hub binary is older than CLI
given: CLI version is 26.3 and hex-hub binary is 26.2
when: User runs 'hex hub status' or 'hex status'
then: Output includes a warning: 'hex-hub 26.2 is outdated (CLI is 26.3). Run hex setup to rebuild.'

given: CLI version 26.3 and hex-hub binary version 26.3

description: hex dashboard prefers Rust binary over Node fallback
given: Rust hex-hub binary exists at target/release/hex-hub
then: Launches the Rust binary via HubLauncher, NOT the Node DashboardHub

description: hex dashboard falls back to Node when no Rust binary
given: No Rust hex-hub binary found on any path
then: Falls back to the Node.js DashboardHub with a warning message

description: Running Node daemon is replaced by Rust binary on restart
given: A Node.js dashboard daemon is running AND a Rust binary is available
then: Stops the Node daemon and starts the Rust binary

description: hex status shows both CLI and hub versions
